_comment: Validated-code dictionary for the Cg recipe library. Every entry compiles + simulates + synthesizes (yosys). kind=primitive -> general/customer-reusable; kind=example -> illustrative composition (one domain). Merge examples/*.cg into the kit; fold this metadata into _RECIPE_META.

entries.name: Recip
entries.use_when: 1/d reciprocal of a runtime value (no hardware divider)
entries.adapt: change numerator scaling; widen for more fractional bits
entries.note: bit-serial long division (digit recurrence); literal shifts only; unrolled combinational

entries.name: Divide
entries.use_when: general a/b division of runtime operands
entries.adapt: swap the numerator pre-shift for the output Q-format

entries.name: SeqDiv
entries.use_when: area-cheap divider when latency is acceptable (one stage reused over N cycles)
entries.adapt: change bit count / Q-format; same FSM
entries.note: rate-changing port I/O (read-when-idle, write-when-done); ~7x smaller than combinational Divide, ~48x latency

entries.name: FixedSqrt
entries.adapt: feed your value as x; keep the literal-shift isqrt loop

entries.name: RSqrt
entries.adapt: isqrt then bit-serial reciprocal; both literal-shift

entries.name: SqrDist
entries.use_when: weighted squared-difference reduction with REAL input ports (datapath survives synthesis)
entries.adapt: per-element op (weights via fxmul(w,fxmul(d,d))); keep in-push ports + driver

entries.name: DotProduct
entries.adapt: change per-element op via fxmul; add input vectors

entries.name: Fir
entries.use_when: streaming FIR filter; sample shift-register is state
entries.note: stateful streaming (one sample in/out per cycle)

entries.name: Integ
entries.use_when: Euler integrator v+=a*dt; x+=v*dt; stateful pos/vel time loop
entries.adapt: change the update rule / add leapfrog half-steps

entries.name: Distance
entries.use_when: Euclidean distance r = sqrt(sum (a-b)^2) (pre-composed MAC + isqrt)
entries.adapt: change per-term op or vector length; keep the isqrt

entries.name: Counter
entries.use_when: sequential state -> a registered output that advances each cycle
entries.adapt: change the update rule in loop(); widen the type for bigger ranges
entries.note: registered-output phase: first value out is the reset value

entries.name: BarrelShift
entries.use_when: shift by a RUNTIME amount (Cg cannot emit a variable shift) — a mux tree of literal shifts
entries.adapt: drop the arms you don't need; widen the value/amount; SRA uses i32, SLL/SRL stay u32
entries.note: the canonical workaround for 'shift by a variable' — how barrel shifters are built in RTL (literal power-of-two shifts gated by each shift-amount bit)

entries.name: RegisterFile
entries.use_when: an array addressed by a runtime index, with a hardwired-zero entry and write-first reads
entries.adapt: change entry count/width; drop the x0 guard if no hardwired zero; add or remove read ports
entries.note: runtime-indexed read AND write; entry 0 hardwired to 0 via guards (no real cell); write applied before reads

entries.name: BitFieldDecode
entries.use_when: extract a bit field from a word and sign-extend it (shift+mask, reassemble scattered bits, cast through a narrow signed type)
entries.adapt: change field positions/widths; a U-type-style upper-field needs no sign extension
entries.note: sign-extend by casting through i<N> of the field's natural width, then up to i32 (the sign bit replicates)

entries.name: Tee2
entries.use_when: replicate ONE push stream to two consumers (a bare push-output fan-out deadlocks the sim)
entries.adapt: add y2/y3... output ports for more consumers; one Tee per fan-out point
entries.note: explicit 1->2 replicator — the fix for the push-output fan-out deadlock (see reference_cg_push_fanout_deadlock)

entries.name: Clamp
entries.use_when: saturate/clip a value to a [lo,hi] range (overflow guard, range limit)
entries.adapt: fix lo/hi as constants for a one-sided limiter; widen the type as needed

entries.name: Lerp
entries.use_when: linear interpolation y = a + (b-a)*t, Q16.16, t in [0,1] (blend/ramp/crossfade)
entries.adapt: clamp t to [0,1] first; run one per channel to interpolate vectors

entries.name: FifoPipe
entries.use_when: buffer/decouple a producer and consumer with an elastic queue (one clock domain)
entries.adapt: change {size,width}; for two clock domains use std.fifo.AsynchronousFIFO with a clocks property
entries.note: std.fifo.SynchronousFIFO: `new ...({size,width})`; wire `fifo.reads(producer.dout)` + `consumer.reads(fifo.dout)`. Both ports are `stream` so the handshake auto-stalls (write blocks when full, read blocks when empty); the FIFO holds each element until read. Verified on BOTH bytecode and iverilog.
